content.content:
127→func (o *fanoutOrchestrator) executeBranch(ctx context.Context, branch BranchSpec) BranchResult {
   128→	startTime := time.Now()
   129→
   130→	result := BranchResult{
   131→		ID:        branch.ID,
   132→		Status:    "failed", // Default to failed
   133→		StartedAt: startTime,
   134→	}
   135→
   136→	// Check if context is already cancelled
   137→	select {
   138→	case <-ctx.Done():
   139→		result.Status = "cancelled"
   140→		result.Notes = "context cancelled"
   141→		result.FinishedAt = time.Now()
   142→		result.Duration = time.Since(startTime)
   143→		return result
   144→	default:
   145→	}
   146→
   147→	// Check if this is a test submitter (backward compatibility)
   148→	if testSubmitter, ok := o.submitter.(interface {
   149→		SubmitAndWaitTerminal(ctx context.Context, spec JobSpec) (JobResult, error)
   150→	}); ok {
   151→		spec := JobSpec{
   152→			Name:    branch.ID,
   153→			Type:    branch.Type,
   154→			Inputs:  branch.Inputs,
   155→			Timeout: 30 * time.Minute, // Default timeout
   156→		}
   157→
   158→		jobResult, err := testSubmitter.SubmitAndWaitTerminal(ctx, spec)
   159→		result.FinishedAt = time.Now()
   160→		result.Duration = time.Since(startTime)
   161→
   162→		if err != nil {
   163→			result.Status = "failed"
   164→			result.Notes = fmt.Sprintf("job execution failed: %v", err)
   165→		} else {
   166→			result.JobID = jobResult.JobID
   167→			result.Status = jobResult.Status
   168→			result.Notes = jobResult.Output
   169→		}
   170→
   171→		return result
   172→	}
   173→
   174→	// Production implementation using real Nomad job submission
   175→	if o.runner != nil {
   176→		switch branch.Type {
   177→		case "llm-exec":
   178→			return o.executeLLMExecBranch(ctx, branch, result)
   179→		case "orw-gen":
   180→			return o.executeORWGenBranch(ctx, branch, result)
   181→		case "human-step":
   182→			return o.executeHumanStepBranch(ctx, branch, result)
   183→		default:
   184→			result.Status = "failed"
   185→			result.Notes = fmt.Sprintf("unsupported branch type: %s", branch.Type)
   186→			result.FinishedAt = time.Now()
   187→			result.Duration = time.Since(startTime)
   188→			return result
   189→		}
   190→	}
   191→
   192→	// No runner provided and not a test submitter
   193→	result.Status = "failed"
   194→	result.Notes = "no production runner or test submitter available for job submission"
   195→	result.FinishedAt = time.Now()
   196→	result.Duration = time.Since(startTime)
   197→	return result
   198→}
   199→
   200→// executeLLMExecBranch executes an LLM-based code generation branch
   201→func (o *fanoutOrchestrator) executeLLMExecBranch(ctx context.Context, branch BranchSpec, result BranchResult) BranchResult {
   202→	// Step 1: Render LLM exec assets
   203→	hclPath, err := o.runner.RenderLLMExecAssets(branch.ID)
   204→	if err != nil {
   205→		result.Status = "failed"
   206→		result.Notes = fmt.Sprintf("failed to render LLM exec assets: %v", err)
   207→		result.FinishedAt = time.Now()
   208→		result.Duration = time.Since(result.StartedAt)
   209→		return result
   210→	}
   211→
   212→	// Step 2: Generate unique run ID for this branch
   213→	runID := fmt.Sprintf("llm-exec-%s-%d", branch.ID, time.Now().Unix())
   214→
   215→	// Step 3: Substitute environment variables in HCL template
   216→	renderedHCLPath, err := substituteHCLTemplate(hclPath, runID)
   217→	if err != nil {
   218→		result.Status = "failed"
   219→		result.Notes = fmt.Sprintf("failed to substitute HCL template: %v", err)
   220→		result.FinishedAt = time.Now()
   221→		result.Duration = time.Since(result.StartedAt)
   222→		return result
   223→	}
   224→
   225→	// Step 4: Submit job to Nomad and wait for completion
   226→	timeout := 30 * time.Minute

260→		result.Duration = time.Since(result.StartedAt)
   261→		return result
   262→	}
   263→
   264→	// Step 2: Read HCL template and substitute recipe-specific variables
   265→	hclBytes, err := os.ReadFile(hclPath)
   266→	if err != nil {
   267→		result.Status = "failed"
   268→		result.Notes = fmt.Sprintf("failed to read ORW HCL template: %v", err)
   269→		result.FinishedAt = time.Now()
   270→		result.Duration = time.Since(result.StartedAt)
   271→		return result
   272→	}
   273→
   274→	// Get recipe configuration from branch inputs
   275→	rclass := ""
   276→	rcoords := ""
   277→	rtimeout := "10m"
   278→
   279→	if inputs, ok := branch.Inputs["recipe_config"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
   280→		if class, ok := inputs["class"].(string); ok {
   281→			rclass = class
   282→		}
   283→		if coords, ok := inputs["coords"].(string); ok {
   284→			rcoords = coords
   285→		}
   286→		if timeout, ok := inputs["timeout"].(string); ok {
   287→			rtimeout = timeout
   288→		}
   289→	}
   290→
   291→	// Perform recipe-specific substitution
   292→	rendered := strings.NewReplacer(
   293→		"${RECIPE_CLASS}", rclass,
   294→		"${RECIPE_COORDS}", rcoords,
   295→		"${RECIPE_TIMEOUT}", rtimeout,
   296→	).Replace(string(hclBytes))
   297→
   298→	// Write substituted HCL to a new file
   299→	renderedHCLPath := strings.ReplaceAll(hclPath, ".rendered.hcl", ".rendered.submitted.hcl")
   300→	if err := os.WriteFile(renderedHCLPath, []byte(rendered), 0644); err != nil {
   301→		result.Status = "failed"
   302→		result.Notes = fmt.Sprintf("failed to write substituted ORW HCL: %v", err)
   303→		result.FinishedAt = time.Now()
   304→		result.Duration = time.Since(result.StartedAt)
   305→		return result
   306→	}
   307→
   308→	// Step 3: Submit job to Nomad and wait for completion
   309→	timeout := 30 * time.Minute
   310→	if err := orchestration.SubmitAndWaitTerminal(renderedHCLPath, timeout); err != nil {
   311→		result.Status = "failed"
   312→		result.Notes = fmt.Sprintf("ORW apply job failed: %v", err)
   313→		result.FinishedAt = time.Now()
   314→		result.Duration = time.Since(result.StartedAt)
   315→		return result
   316→	}
   317→
   318→	// Step 4: Check for generated diff.patch artifact
   319→	diffPath := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(renderedHCLPath), "out", "diff.patch")
   320→	if _, err := os.Stat(diffPath); err != nil {
   321→		result.Status = "failed"
   322→		result.Notes = fmt.Sprintf("ORW apply job completed but no diff.patch found: %v", err)
   323→		result.FinishedAt = time.Now()
   324→		result.Duration = time.Since(result.StartedAt)
   325→		return result
   326→	}
   327→
   328→	result.Status = "completed"
   329→	result.Notes = fmt.Sprintf("ORW apply job completed successfully, diff.patch at: %s", diffPath)
   330→	result.FinishedAt = time.Now()
   331→	result.Duration = time.Since(result.StartedAt)
   332→	return result
   333→}
   334→
   335→// executeHumanStepBranch handles human intervention branches with Git-based manual intervention workflow
   336→func (o *fanoutOrchestrator) executeHumanStepBranch(ctx context.Context, branch BranchSpec, result BranchResult) BranchResult {
   337→	// Parse timeout from branch inputs
   338→	timeout := 30 * time.Minute // default timeout
   339→	if timeoutStr, ok := branch.Inputs["timeout"].(string); ok {
   340→		if parsedTimeout, err := time.ParseDuration(timeoutStr); err == nil {
   341→			timeout = parsedTimeout
   342→		}
   343→	}
   344→
   345→	// Create timeout context for this branch
   346→	branchCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, timeout)
   347→	defer cancel()
   348→
   349→	// Get build error context
   350→	buildError, _ := branch.Inputs["buildError"].(string)
   351→	if buildError == "" {
   352→		buildError = "Build failure - human intervention required"
   353→	}
   354→
   355→	// Check if runner is available for production mode
   356→	if o.runner == nil {
   357→		result.Status = "failed"
   358→		result.Notes = "human-step branches require production runner (not available in test mode)"
   359→		result.FinishedAt = time.Now()

content.text: I'll consolidate and organize the work logs for the "Complete Healing Branch Types" task. Let me first read the task file and then the transcript to understand what was accomplished.

content.content:
1→---
     2→task: m-implement-complete-healing-branch-types
     3→branch: feature/implement-complete-healing-branch-types
     4→status: in-progress
     5→created: 2025-09-05
     6→started: 2025-09-05
     7→modules: [internal/cli/transflow, roadmap/transflow, tests]
     8→---
     9→
    10→# Complete Healing Branch Types Implementation
    11→
    12→## Problem/Goal
    13→The transflow healing infrastructure has production Nomad job submission working, but the healing branch types need completion. Currently, human-step branches are just placeholders, and comprehensive testing coverage is needed for all branch types (llm-exec, orw-gen, human-step). Additionally, the roadmap needs to be updated to reflect completed tasks and follow TDD principles from @AGENTS.md.
    14→
    15→## Success Criteria
    16→- [x] Implement complete human-step branch handler with Git-based manual intervention workflow
    17→- [x] Ensure llm-exec branch type is fully functional with MCP tool integration
    18→- [x] Verify orw-gen branch type works with OpenRewrite recipe generation and application
    19→- [x] Add comprehensive test coverage for all three healing branch types
    20→- [x] Update roadmap/transflow/MVP.md to mark completed tasks and current status
    21→- [x] Follow TDD framework from @AGENTS.md: RED (failing tests) → GREEN (minimal code) → REFACTOR (VPS testing)
    22→- [x] Deploy to VPS for integration testing following mandatory update protocol
    23→- [x] Update FEATURES.md and CHANGELOG.md as needed
    24→
    25→## Context Manifest
    26→
    27→### How This Currently Works: Transflow Self-Healing Architecture
    28→
    29→The transflow healing infrastructure is a sophisticated multi-phase workflow system that automatically recovers from build failures using parallel healing strategies. When a transflow execution encounters a build failure, it triggers a three-phase healing process: **planner → fanout → reducer**.
    30→
    31→#### Phase 1: Planner Job Analysis
    32→
    33→When a build fails during the normal transflow workflow (clone → branch → recipes → commit → **build failure**), the `attemptHealing` method in `runner.go` is called with the build error message. The healing process begins by creating a `TransflowHealingSummary` to track the entire healing attempt.
    34→
    35→The system first submits a **planner job** via `JobSubmissionHelper.SubmitPlannerJob()`. This planner is a Nomad batch job that analyzes the build error context and generates healing options. The planner job runs in a Docker container with the LangGraph planner model, receiving:
    36→- Build error details and context
    37→- Repository metadata and state
    38→- Knowledge base snapshots (future enhancement)
    39→
    40→The planner writes a `plan.json` artifact containing an array of healing options. Each option specifies a different healing strategy with its own type and confidence score.
    41→
    42→#### Phase 2: Fanout Orchestration (Parallel Branch Execution)
    43→
    44→Once the planner completes, the system converts the planner options into `BranchSpec` structures and submits them to the `FanoutOrchestrator`. This orchestrator implements a **first-success-wins** parallel execution model with configurable parallelism limits.
    45→
    46→The fanout orchestrator launches multiple healing "branches" simultaneously:
    47→- **llm-exec**: LLM-powered code generation that analyzes the error and generates a patch
    48→- **orw-gen**: OpenRewrite recipe generation that suggests and applies automated refactoring recipes  
    49→- **human-step**: Manual intervention workflow that creates Git branches/MRs for human review
    50→
    51→Each branch executes as an independent Nomad job with its own resource allocation and timeout. The orchestrator uses Go channels and context cancellation to coordinate the parallel execution. When the first branch succeeds, it cancels all remaining branches to prevent resource waste.
    52→
    53→#### Phase 3: Reducer Decision Making
    54→
    55→After branch execution completes (either with a winner or all failures), the system submits a **reducer job** via `JobSubmissionHelper.SubmitReducerJob()`. The reducer analyzes all branch results and the winning solution to determine the next action.
    56→
    57→The reducer typically returns `{"action": "stop", "notes": "healing succeeded"}` when a branch successfully fixes the build, allowing the main transflow workflow to continue with the healed changes.
    58→
    59→#### Branch Type Implementations (Current State)
    60→
    61→**llm-exec branches** (`executeLLMExecBranch` in `fanout_orchestrator.go`):
    62→- Renders LLM execution assets using `RenderLLMExecAssets()` 
    63→- Substitutes environment variables in the HCL template (MODEL, TOOLS_JSON, LIMITS_JSON, RUN_ID)
    64→- Submits as Nomad job using `orchestration.SubmitAndWaitTerminal()`
    65→- Expects the job to produce a `diff.patch` artifact in the output directory
    66→- Validates success by checking for the diff.patch file existence
    67→
    68→**orw-gen branches** (`executeORWGenBranch`):
    69→- Renders OpenRewrite application assets using `RenderORWApplyAssets()`
    70→- Extracts recipe configuration from branch inputs (class, coords, timeout)
    71→- Performs recipe-specific HCL variable substitution (RECIPE_CLASS, RECIPE_COORDS, RECIPE_TIMEOUT)
    72→- Submits as Nomad job and waits for completion
    73→- Validates success by checking for generated diff.patch artifact
    74→
    75→**human-step branches** (`executeHumanStepBranch`):
    76→- Currently returns immediate failure with "not yet implemented" message
    77→- Intended to create Git branches/MRs for manual intervention
    78→- Should poll for manual commits and validate build success after human changes
    79→
    80→### For New Feature Implementation: Completing the Missing Pieces
    81→
    82→Since we're implementing complete healing branch types with comprehensive test coverage, several critical integration points need completion:
    83→
    84→#### Human-Step Branch Implementation Requirements
    85→
    86→The current human-step implementation is a placeholder that immediately fails. The complete implementation needs to:
    87→
    88→1. **Git Branch Creation**: Create a dedicated Git branch for human intervention, separate from the main workflow branch
    89→2. **Issue/MR Creation**: Use the existing `GitProvider` interface to create GitLab merge requests with detailed error context
    90→3. **Polling Mechanism**: Implement a polling loop that checks for manual commits on the human intervention branch
    91→4. **Build Validation**: After detecting manual changes, re-run the build check to verify the fix
    92→5. **Timeout Handling**: Respect branch-level timeouts and gracefully fail if human intervention doesn't occur
    93→
    94→The human-step workflow should integrate with the existing `r.gitProvider` (GitLab integration) and `r.buildChecker` interfaces already available in the `TransflowRunner`.
    95→
    96→#### Test Coverage Completion
    97→
    98→The existing test infrastructure in `job_submission_test.go` and `self_healing_test.go` uses a `MockJobSubmitter` pattern that simulates job execution. The missing test coverage needs to address:
    99→
   100→1. **Branch Type Validation**: Tests for each branch type (llm-exec, orw-gen, human-step) with both success and failure scenarios
   101→2. **Timeout Handling**: Tests that verify proper timeout behavior and context cancellation
   102→3. **Error Recovery**: Tests that validate error handling when branches fail at different stages
   103→4. **Integration Testing**: End-to-end tests that combine planner → fanout → reducer workflows
   104→
   105→The test infrastructure already provides `MockJobSubmitter` with configurable `JobResults` mapping and error injection capabilities.
   106→
   107→#### Production Job Submission Integration
   108→
   109→The healing workflow integrates with the existing Nomad job submission infrastructure through the `orchestration.SubmitAndWaitTerminal()` function. The production flow:
   110→
   111→1. **Asset Rendering**: Uses `RenderLLMExecAssets()`, `RenderORWApplyAssets()` to prepare HCL job templates
   112→2. **Variable Substitution**: Performs environment variable substitution in HCL templates using `substituteHCLTemplate()`
   113→3. **Job Submission**: Calls `orchestration.SubmitAndWaitTerminal()` with rendered HCL paths and timeout values
   114→4. **Artifact Collection**: Reads job output artifacts (diff.patch files) from expected output directories
   115→5. **Result Processing**: Parses artifacts and determines branch success/failure
   116→
   117→The existing HCL templates in `roadmap/transflow/jobs/` define the Nomad job specifications with placeholder variables that get substituted at runtime.
   118→
   119→### Technical Reference Details
   120→
   121→#### Component Interfaces & Signatures
   122→
   123→```go
   124→// Main healing orchestration entry point
   125→func (r *TransflowRunner) attemptHealing(ctx context.Context, repoPath string, buildError string) (*TransflowHealingSummary, error)
   126→
   127→// Job submission interfaces
   128→type JobSubmissionHelper interface {
   129→    SubmitPlannerJob(ctx context.Context, config *TransflowConfig, buildError string, workspace string) (*PlanResult, error)
   130→    SubmitReducerJob(ctx context.Context, planID string, results []BranchResult, winner *BranchResult, workspace string) (*NextAction, error)
   131→}
   132→
   133→// Fanout orchestration
   134→type FanoutOrchestrator interface {
   135→    RunHealingFanout(ctx context.Context, runCtx interface{}, branches []BranchSpec, maxParallel int) (BranchResult, []BranchResult, error)
   136→}
   137→
   138→// Branch execution methods
   139→func (o *fanoutOrchestrator) executeLLMExecBranch(ctx context.Context, branch BranchSpec, result BranchResult) BranchResult
   140→func (o *fanoutOrchestrator) executeORWGenBranch(ctx context.Context, branch BranchSpec, result BranchResult) BranchResult
   141→func (o *fanoutOrchestrator) executeHumanStepBranch(ctx context.Context, branch BranchSpec, result BranchResult) BranchResult
   142→```
   143→
   144→#### Data Structures
   145→
   146→```go
   147→// Healing summary tracking
   148→type TransflowHealingSummary struct {
   149→    Enabled       bool                       `json:"enabled"`
   150→    AttemptsCount int                        `json:"attempts_count"`
   151→    MaxRetries    int                        `json:"max_retries"`
   152→    Attempts      []*TransflowHealingAttempt `json:"attempts"`
   153→    FinalSuccess  bool                       `json:"final_success"`
   154→    PlanID        string                     `json:"plan_id,omitempty"`
   155→    Winner        *BranchResult              `json:"winner,omitempty"`
   156→    AllResults    []BranchResult             `json:"all_results,omitempty"`
   157→}
   158→
   159→// Branch specifications and results
   160→type BranchSpec struct {
   161→    ID     string                 `json:"id"`
   162→    Type   string                 `json:"type"`   // "llm-exec", "orw-gen", "human-step"
   163→    Inputs map[string]interface{} `json:"inputs"`
   164→}
   165→
   166→type BranchResult struct {
   167→    ID         string        `json:"id"`
   168→    Status     string        `json:"status"`     // "completed", "failed", "timeout", "cancelled"
   169→    JobID      string        `json:"job_id"`
   170→    Notes      string        `json:"notes"`
   171→    StartedAt  time.Time     `json:"started_at"`
   172→    FinishedAt time.Time     `json:"finished_at"`
   173→    Duration   time.Duration `json:"duration"`
   174→}
   175→```
   176→
   177→#### Configuration Requirements
   178→
   179→```yaml
   180→# transflow.yaml self-healing configuration
   181→self_heal:
   182→  enabled: true
   183→  max_retries: 2      # Maximum healing attempts (1-5)
   184→  cooldown: 30s       # Optional cooldown between attempts
   185→```
   186→
   187→Environment variables for healing jobs:
   188→- `TRANSFLOW_MODEL`: LLM model for healing (default: gpt-4o-mini@2024-08-06)
   189→- `TRANSFLOW_TOOLS`: MCP tools configuration JSON
   190→- `TRANSFLOW_LIMITS`: Execution limits configuration JSON
   191→
   192→#### File Locations
   193→
   194→- **Main Implementation**: 
   195→  - `internal/cli/transflow/runner.go` - Main healing orchestration in `attemptHealing()`
   196→  - `internal/cli/transflow/fanout_orchestrator.go` - Branch execution logic (needs human-step completion)
   197→  - `internal/cli/transflow/job_submission.go` - Nomad job submission helpers
   198→
   199→- **Test Files**:
   200→  - `internal/cli/transflow/self_healing_test.go` - Healing workflow tests (needs branch-specific coverage)
   201→  - `internal/cli/transflow/job_submission_test.go` - Job submission tests (needs human-step tests)
   202→  - `internal/cli/transflow/integration_test.go` - End-to-end integration tests
   203→
   204→- **Nomad Job Templates**:
   205→  - `roadmap/transflow/jobs/llm_exec.hcl` - LLM execution job template
   206→  - `roadmap/transflow/jobs/orw_apply.hcl` - OpenRewrite application job template
   207→  - Need to create: `roadmap/transflow/jobs/human_step.hcl` (if job-based approach is used)
   208→
   209→- **Documentation Updates**:
   210→  - `roadmap/transflow/MVP.md` - Update implementation status from "Partially Implemented" to "Fully Implemented"
   211→  - `FEATURES.md` - Document completed healing branch types
   212→  - `CHANGELOG.md` - Record implementation completion
   213→
   214→## User Notes
   215→- Follow @AGENTS.md TDD framework strictly - write failing tests first, minimal implementation, then VPS refactor phase
   216→- Human-step branch should involve creating Git branches/MRs and polling for manual commits
   217→- All branch types should have proper error handling and timeout management
   218→- Tests should cover both success and failure scenarios for each branch type
   219→- Integration with existing Nomad job submission infrastructure is already complete
   220→

   222→- [2025-09-05] Created task for completing healing branch types implementation
